en.extract:
The garnet robin (Eugerygone rubra) is a species of bird in the family Petroicidae. It is monotypic within the genus Eugerygone. It is found in New Guinea, where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy
The garnet robin was described by the English ornithologist, Richard Bowdler Sharpe, in 1879, from a specimen collected in the Arfak Mountains on the island of New Guinea. He coined the binomial name Pseudogerygone rubra. It was moved to the genus Eugerygone by the German naturalist, Otto Finsch, in 1901.

The '''garnet robin''' ('''''Eugerygone rubra''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Petroicidae]]. It is [[monotypic]] within the genus '''''Eugerygone'''''.<ref name = "ITIS"/> It is found in [[New Guinea]], where its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

==Taxonomy==
The garnet robin was described by the English ornithologist, [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe]], in 1879, from a specimen collected in the [[Arfak Mountains]] on the island of [[New Guinea]]. He coined the [[binomial name]] ''Pseudogerygone rubra''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Sharpe | first=Richard Bowdler | author-link=Richard Bowdler Sharpe | year=1879 | title=''Pseudogerygone rubra'' | journal=Notes from the Leyden Museum | volume=1 | pages=29–30 | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/9630908 }}</ref> It was moved to the genus ''Eugerygone'' by the German naturalist, [[Otto Finsch]], in 1901.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Finsch | first=Otto | author-link=Otto Finsch | year=1901 | title=Zur catalogisirung der ornithologischen abtheilung  | language=de | journal=Notes from the Leyden Museum | volume=22 | issue=3 | pages=193–224 [200] | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/9646257 }}</ref>
